name: Fox
summary: Red foxes are quick, clever wild dogs with bushy tails and excellent hearing. They hunt mice and rabbits, raise kits in dens, and live from Arctic tundra to...
metaTitle: Fox Facts | Habitat, Diet, Kits & Photos
metaDescription: Discover red fox facts with photos, forest habitat, hunting diet, kit life, and behavior. Learn where foxes live, what they eat, and how they hunt at night.
searchIntents: what is a baby fox called

lifespan.captivity: Up to 14 years in human care

speed: Up to 30 mph in short bursts
funFacts: A fox uses its bushy tail for balance and warmth.
Foxes can hear rodents moving underground.
Red foxes have a wide range of coat colors, including black and silver.
Fox kits play-fight to practice hunting skills.
A group of foxes is sometimes called a skulk or leash.
Foxes store extra food by burying it to eat later.

faq.question: What is a fox?
faq.answer: A fox is a small wild member of the dog family. The red fox is the most widespread species and lives on several continents.

faq.question: Where do foxes live?
faq.answer: Red foxes live in forests, grasslands, farms, deserts, and even city parks across Europe, Asia, North America, and parts of Africa.

faq.question: What do foxes eat?
faq.answer: Foxes eat mice, voles, rabbits, birds, insects, fruit, and other food they can catch or find.

faq.question: What is a baby fox called?
faq.answer: A baby fox is called a kit or cub. Kits are born in an underground den.

faq.question: Are foxes nocturnal?
faq.answer: Foxes are most active at night and at dawn or dusk, though they may come out in daylight too.

faq.question: How are foxes different from wolves?
faq.answer: Foxes are much smaller, usually hunt alone, and eat smaller prey. Wolves live in packs and hunt large animals.

faq.question: Are foxes endangered?
faq.answer: Red foxes are listed as Least Concern and remain common in many regions, though local populations can still face threats.
